query: what causes the aurora borealis colors

text_blocks.snippet: The colors of the northern lights depend on the type of gas being excited by charged solar particles and the altitude of the collision. They reveal real-time atmospheric chemistry rather than holding symbolic or spiritual meaning:
snippet_highlighted_words: depend on the type of gas being excited by charged solar particles and the altitude of the collision

list.snippet: Green (Most Common): Caused by solar particles colliding with oxygen at altitudes of 60 to 190 miles (100 to 300 km).

![What are the Northern Lights? And how can you see them? - BBC ...](https://api.dataforseo.com/cdn/i/07210109-2120-0139-0000-fdef2af7bbb6:22)
![Understanding aurora colors; the science behind the display ...](https://api.dataforseo.com/cdn/i/07210109-2120-0139-0000-fdef2af7bbb6:23)
![How Cameras Reveal the Northern Lights' True Colors (Op-Ed ...](https://api.dataforseo.com/cdn/i/07210109-2120-0139-0000-fdef2af7bbb6:24)

The colors of the northern lights `depend on the type of gas being excited by charged solar particles and the altitude of the collision` . They reveal real-time atmospheric chemistry rather than holding symbolic or spiritual meaning:[](https://www.facebook.com/100044470387919/posts/aurora-color-guide-if-you-are-seeing-red-or-green-these-auroras-are-at-an-altitu/1391405102351823/) [[1]](https://www.facebook.com/100044470387919/posts/aurora-color-guide-if-you-are-seeing-red-or-green-these-auroras-are-at-an-altitu/1391405102351823/)[[2]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aurora)[[3]](https://staylapland.com/blogs/why-do-northern-lights-appear-in-different-colors/)

- **Green (Most Common):** Caused by solar particles colliding with oxygen at altitudes of 60 to 190 miles (100 to 300 km).
- **Red (Rare):** Caused by high-altitude oxygen interactions above 150 miles (240 km), typically indicating intense solar storm activity.
- **Blue and Purple:** Caused by energized nitrogen molecules interacting with solar winds, typically seen at lower altitudes.
- **Pink and Magenta:** Created when lower-level nitrogen is excited, often appearing as a colorful border beneath a green aurora curtain.[](https://theaurorazone.com/about-the-aurora/the-science-of-the-northern-lights/the-northern-lights-colours/) [[1]](https://theaurorazone.com/about-the-aurora/the-science-of-the-northern-lights/the-northern-lights-colours/)[[2]](https://science.nasa.gov/sun/auroras/)[[3]](https://www.space.com/15139-northern-lights-auroras-earth-facts-sdcmp.html)[[4]](https://staylapland.com/blogs/why-do-northern-lights-appear-in-different-colors/)

For more detailed science on atmospheric composition and space weather, you can read the [NASA Science Aurora Overview](https://science.nasa.gov/sun/auroras/) or check the [Explore Fairbanks Guide](https://www.explorefairbanks.com/blog/post/northern-lights-colors-what-do-they-mean/) to auroral displays.

The colors of the northern lights `depend on the type of gas being excited by charged solar particles and the altitude of the collision` . They reveal real-time atmospheric chemistry rather than holding symbolic or spiritual meaning:[](https://www.facebook.com/100044470387919/posts/aurora-color-guide-if-you-are-seeing-red-or-green-these-auroras-are-at-an-altitu/1391405102351823/) [[1]](https://www.facebook.com/100044470387919/posts/aurora-color-guide-if-you-are-seeing-red-or-green-these-auroras-are-at-an-altitu/1391405102351823/)[[2]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aurora)[[3]](https://staylapland.com/blogs/why-do-northern-lights-appear-in-different-colors/)

- **Green (Most Common):** Caused by solar particles colliding with oxygen at altitudes of 60 to 190 miles (100 to 300 km).
- **Red (Rare):** Caused by high-altitude oxygen interactions above 150 miles (240 km), typically indicating intense solar storm activity.
- **Blue and Purple:** Caused by energized nitrogen molecules interacting with solar winds, typically seen at lower altitudes.
- **Pink and Magenta:** Created when lower-level nitrogen is excited, often appearing as a colorful border beneath a green aurora curtain.[](https://theaurorazone.com/about-the-aurora/the-science-of-the-northern-lights/the-northern-lights-colours/) [[1]](https://theaurorazone.com/about-the-aurora/the-science-of-the-northern-lights/the-northern-lights-colours/)[[2]](https://science.nasa.gov/sun/auroras/)[[3]](https://www.space.com/15139-northern-lights-auroras-earth-facts-sdcmp.html)[[4]](https://staylapland.com/blogs/why-do-northern-lights-appear-in-different-colors/)
